id: Cloud Top Height (CTH) by Sentinel-5P TROPOMI (Daily)
description:
## Cloud Top Height (CTH) by Sentinel-5P TROPOMI (Daily)

The Cloud Top Height (CTH) measures the **altitude of the top of cloud layers** above the Earth’s surface, providing key information for understanding cloud vertical structure and its impact on radiation, weather, and atmospheric processes.

Cloud height data from Sentinel-5P is crucial for satellite trace gas retrieval corrections, as clouds strongly affect the measured atmospheric signal. It also contributes to extending long-term satellite cloud records, enhancing climate and weather studies.

Unlike columnar aerosol measures or aerosol layer heights, CTH focuses on **cloud vertical positioning**, not particle concentration. High clouds (e.g., cirrus) and low clouds (e.g., stratocumulus) have very different radiative impacts and interactions with atmospheric dynamics.

#### About the dataset

- The CTH product is derived from measurements by the [TROPOMI instrument](https://www.tropomi.eu/) aboard Copernicus ESA's [Sentinel-5P Precursor](https://www.esa.int/Applications/Observing_the_Earth/Copernicus/Sentinel-5P) satellite.


#### Use cases
The CTH product, combined with the [Cloud Fraction (CF)](https://eodashboard.org/explore/?x=0.0000&y=0.0000&z=2.5934&template=expert&indicator=S5P-L3GRD-CLOUDFR-DAY&datetime=2025-01-02) product, is particularly valuable for understanding the role of clouds in weather, climate, and atmospheric composition studies and for operational applications in satellite-based monitoring. Some of applications of CTH products are:
- Improving atmospheric trace gas retrievals by correcting for cloud contamination
- Monitoring cloud vertical structure and distribution
- Climate research: radiative forcing, cloud–radiation interactions
- Weather studies: cloud coverage and convection patterns
